steps.title: Locate the verb and object
steps.content: The sentence has the subject “Her innovative poems,” the verb “popularized,” and the direct object “the use of Jamaican Creole.” Standard English does not put punctuation between a transitive verb and its object.

steps.title: Keep the object attached
steps.content: Choice A inserts a colon between the verb and object. Choice B inserts a comma, and Choice D inserts a semicolon. All three wrongly interrupt the verb-object pair, so the sentence needs no punctuation after “popularized.” The answer is C.
